reforms.description: Switzerland proposed a Corporate Tax Reform III package that includes the introduction of a patent box and super-deductions for R&D expenditures, aimed at aligning with international tax rules and enhancing the competitiveness of the tax environment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The introduction of a patent box and super-deductions for R&D expenditures is expected to enhance the competitiveness of the tax environment, thereby supporting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been passed into law but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the corporate tax framework, aimed at enhancing R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: The resulting Corporate Tax Reform III package... is a comprehensive set of measures aiming to ensure alignment with the international tax rules.

reforms.description: Public energy R&D funding was stepped up by 25% to CHF 250 million per annum in 2014, aimed at enhancing energy-related research and facilitating the realization of the Energy Strategy 2050.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for energy R&D is expected to enhance research capabilities and support the transition to sustainable energy solutions, contributing to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding increase has been successfully implemented since 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding increase is significant but is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Public energy R&D funding was stepped up 25% to CHF 250 million per annum in 2014.
